goal: find the start_pos concretizer that bakes the opaque append's write offset in the full-model JIT decode replay (only the capture position written).
key_fact: the kv_append PROGRAM's ProgramInfo.vars = {'start_pos'} -> start_pos IS a DECLARED live runtime var on the append. Yet replays don't advance.

hypotheses_tested.rope_producer_bakes_data (user hypothesi: REFUTED -- isolated append with a start_pos-dependent (rope-like) src advances across replays; and the model writes ONLY the capture position (offset baked, not stale data).
hypotheses_tested.@function_prefill: REFUTED -- one @function forward then a fresh decode jit ADVANCES.
hypotheses_tested.prefill_jit (short, canonical): REFUTED -- 3-step canonical prefill jit then decode ADVANCES.
hypotheses_tested.high_start_pos_prefill: REFUTED -- prefill at start_pos 2045/2046/2047 (<2048) then decode ADVANCES.
hypotheses_tested.256_step_prefill: REFUTED -- 256-step prefill from 0 then decode ADVANCES.
hypotheses_tested.cache_uop_RESHAPE_materialization: REFUTED -- cache_kv.uop is Ops.RESHAPE in BOTH advancing and baking cases; re-realize (.contiguous().realize()) keeps RESHAPE and does NOT fix it.
hypotheses_tested.runtime_route_fires_during_prefill: REFUTED -- batched prefill (T>1, canonical, route does NOT fire) still BAKES.

narrowed_trigger: The baking is triggered specifically when the cache is filled via the MODEL'S CANONICAL STORE before the decode jit -- both the full token-by-token (2050-step) prefill AND the batched (T>1) prefill BAKE. But filling the SAME [0:2050] via a direct cache.assign(numpy).realize() ADVANCES. The difference between assign-fill (advances) and canonical-store-fill (bakes) is the buffer/scheduler state left on cache_kv -- both leave cache_kv.uop = RESHAPE, so it is NOT the visible uop op; it is a deeper buffer-identity / pending-store / TinyJit-capture-state difference.
observation: with a canonical-store prefill, the decode jit's append writes ONLY the eager-call (call-0) position; the captured/replayed calls (call-1+) do NOT persist to block.cache_kv -> the captured graph's append does not write the persistent buffer (materializes a fresh one) ONLY in this post-canonical-store-prefill state.
recommended_next: Compare, byte-for-byte, the cache_kv Tensor/UOp/BUFFER identity after canonical-store-fill vs assign-fill (both RESHAPE) -- e.g. cache_kv.uop.base, buffer_identity, any lingering AFTER/STORE in the realize graph. Then either (a) force cache_kv back to a pristine BUFFER identity after the canonical store (detach/clone into a new realized buffer) before the decode jit, or (b) fill the cache via the opaque append during prefill too (needs a short-ctx-correct attention read to avoid the gqa reduce hazard). This is a tinygrad buffer-identity/TinyJit-capture interaction, NOT the RoPE producer.
